# dating.log_time_grid { #tspaint.dating.log_time_grid }

```python
tspaint.dating.log_time_grid(t_min, t_max, n_cells)
```

Geometric (log-spaced) cell edges spanning ``[t_min, t_max]``.

Geometric spacing gives roughly equal expected branch-occupation per cell (the "even power"
placement).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**t_min**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Youngest (smallest) cell edge, in generations. Must be ``> 0`` (geometric spacing).

<code>[**t_max**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Oldest (largest) cell edge, in generations.

<code>[**n_cells**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Number of cells; the grid has ``n_cells + 1`` edges.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(n_cells + 1,)`` ascending cell-boundary **times** (generations), log-spaced — the boundaries are real time values evenly spaced on a log axis, not log-time.
